meta.importedFrom: Unit 3 Passage 2 No I in team（互动课文教学动画）英语新教材外研版八年级下册.html

article.title: No I in team
article.summary: 通过团队故事理解合作、分工和共同目标的意义。

sentences.text: At noon on 24 May 1960, four climbers stood alone on the "roof of the world".
sentences.tr: 1960年5月24日中午，四名登山者独自站在“世界屋脊”上。
sentences.g: 时间状语 + 主语 + 谓语 + 方式状语 + 地点状语
sentences.d: "At noon on 24 May 1960"是具体的时间状语。"roof of the world"指珠穆朗玛峰。

sentences.text: They were near the top of a thirty-metre-high wall of rock.
sentences.tr: 他们靠近一堵三十米高的岩壁顶部。
sentences.d: "thirty-metre-high"是复合形容词，修饰wall。

sentences.text: It was called the Second Step.
sentences.tr: 它被称为第二台阶。
sentences.d: "was called"是被动语态，表示“被称为”。

sentences.text: The top of Mount Qomolangma seemed right within reach.
sentences.tr: 珠穆朗玛峰的顶峰似乎触手可及。
sentences.d: "seemed"是系动词，"within reach"是介词短语作表语，表示“在可及范围内”。

sentences.text: But no one had ever climbed the last few metres of the Second Step before.
sentences.tr: 但以前从未有人攀登过第二台阶的最后几米。
sentences.d: "had climbed"是过去完成时，表示在过去某个时间点之前发生的动作。

sentences.text: Liu Lianman led the way.
sentences.tr: 刘连满带路。
sentences.d: "led"是lead的过去式，意为“带领”。

sentences.text: He tried four times.
sentences.tr: 他尝试了四次。
sentences.g: 主谓结构 + 频度状语
sentences.d: 简单句，描述动作发生的次数。

sentences.text: Each time, he fell back into the snow.
sentences.tr: 每次，他都摔回雪地里。
sentences.g: 时间状语 + 主谓结构 + 地点状语
sentences.d: "fell back"表示“向后摔倒”。

sentences.text: The thin air took his strength away.
sentences.tr: 稀薄的空气耗尽了他的体力。
sentences.d: "took away"是动词短语，意为“带走，耗尽”。

sentences.text: "I'm not strong enough to try again," he thought.
sentences.tr: “我没有足够的力气再试一次了，”他想。
sentences.g: 直接引语 + 主谓结构
sentences.d: "strong enough to do sth"表示“足够强壮去做某事”。

sentences.text: So he chose to be a "human ladder" for his teammates!
sentences.tr: 所以他选择做队友的“人梯”！
sentences.d: "chose to be"表示“选择成为”。"human ladder"是比喻用法。

sentences.text: He lowered his body and let Qu Yinhua stand on his shoulders.
sentences.tr: 他蹲下身子，让屈银华站在他的肩膀上。
sentences.g: 并列谓语 + 使役动词结构
sentences.d: "let sb do sth"表示“让某人做某事”。

sentences.text: Slowly, bit by bit, Liu stood up and helped Qu climb to the top.
sentences.tr: 慢慢地，一点一点地，刘连满站了起来，帮助屈银华爬到了顶部。
sentences.g: 方式状语 + 主谓结构 + 并列谓语
sentences.d: "bit by bit"意为“一点一点地”。

sentences.text: Every second was painful, but Liu stood still.
sentences.tr: 每一秒都很痛苦，但刘连满站着不动。
sentences.d: "stood"在这里系动词用法，"still"是形容词，表示“静止的”。

sentences.text: And he did the same for Gong Bu.
sentences.tr: 他也为贡布做了同样的事。
sentences.d: "the same"指代前面提到的动作（做人梯）。

sentences.text: Then he and Wang Fuzhou climbed up with a rope.
sentences.tr: 然后他和王富洲顺着绳子爬了上去。
sentences.g: 主谓结构 + 方式状语
sentences.d: "with a rope"表示借助绳子。

sentences.text: But Liu was too tired to keep going.
sentences.tr: 但是刘连满太累了，无法继续前进。

sentences.text: The team were worried, but they had to carry on.
sentences.tr: 队员们很担心，但他们必须继续前进。
sentences.d: "had to"表示“不得不，必须”。"carry on"意为“继续”。

sentences.text: They gave Liu all their oxygen.
sentences.tr: 他们把所有的氧气都给了刘连满。
sentences.d: "gave sb sth"表示“给某人某物”。

sentences.text: It was dangerous to climb without oxygen, but they knew Liu needed it.
sentences.tr: 没有氧气攀登很危险，但他们知道刘连满需要它。
sentences.g: 形式主语 + 宾语从句
sentences.d: "It"是形式主语，真正的主语是"to climb without oxygen"。"knew"后省略了that引导的宾语从句。

sentences.text: They made every effort through the cold and dark.
sentences.tr: 他们在寒冷和黑暗中竭尽全力。
sentences.d: "make every effort"意为“竭尽全力”。

sentences.text: Hours later, they reached the top of the towering mountain at 4:20 am on 25 May.
sentences.tr: 几个小时后，他们在5月25日凌晨4点20分到达了这座高耸山峰的顶峰。
sentences.g: 时间状语 + 主谓宾 + 时间状语
sentences.d: "towering"形容词，意为“高耸的”。

sentences.text: They were the first Chinese to do so!
sentences.tr: 他们是第一批这样做的中国人！
sentences.g: 主系表 + 不定式作定语
sentences.d: "to do so"作后置定语修饰"Chinese"。

sentences.text: They were also the first people to climb the difficult North Ridge.
sentences.tr: 他们也是第一批攀登艰难的北坡的人。
sentences.g: 主系表 + 不定式作定语
sentences.d: "to climb..."作后置定语修饰"people"。

sentences.text: What's more, no one had done so without using oxygen!
sentences.tr: 更重要的是，以前从未有人在不使用氧气的情况下这样做过！
sentences.g: 插入语 + 过去完成时
sentences.d: "What's more"表示“更重要的是”。"without using oxygen"是介词短语作方式状语。

sentences.text: When they returned to base camp, they were welcomed as heroes. People cheered for Liu, too.
sentences.tr: 当他们回到大本营时，受到了英雄般的欢迎。人们也为刘连满欢呼。
sentences.g: 时间状语从句 + 被动语态
sentences.d: "were welcomed"是被动语态。

sentences.text: He did not reach the top, but he shouldered his team into China's mountaineering history.
sentences.tr: 他没有到达顶峰，但他用肩膀将他的团队托入了中国登山史。
sentences.d: "shouldered"这里作动词用，双关语，既指“用肩膀托”，也指“肩负”。

readingQuestions.stem: Why did Liu Lianman choose to be a "human ladder"?
options: A. He wanted to show off his strength.
B. He was too tired to climb and wanted to help his teammates.
C. It was the only way to cross a river.
D. He was ordered to do so by the team leader.
readingQuestions.answer: B. He was too tired to climb and wanted to help his teammates.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章提到刘连满尝试了四次都失败了，体力耗尽，所以选择做人梯帮助队友。

readingQuestions.stem: What was special about their achievement?
options: A. They climbed the South Ridge.
B. They were the first people to climb Mount Qomolangma.
C. They were the first Chinese to climb the North Ridge, and did so without oxygen.
D. They found a new route called the Second Step.
readingQuestions.answer: C. They were the first Chinese to climb the North Ridge, and did so without oxygen.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章提到他们是第一批攀登北坡的中国人，且是在没有氧气的情况下完成的。

readingQuestions.stem: What does the phrase "shouldered his team into history" mean?
options: A. He carried his team on his back all the way.
B. He pushed his team off the mountain.
C. His sacrifice and help enabled the team to succeed and make history.
D. He wrote a history book about the team.
readingQuestions.answer: C. His sacrifice and help enabled the team to succeed and make history.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 这是一个比喻，指刘连满的牺牲和帮助（做人梯）使得团队能够成功并创造历史。

readingQuestions.stem: When did the team reach the top of Mount Qomolangma?
options: A. At noon on 24 May 1960.
B. At 4:20 on 25 May.
C. In the afternoon of 24 May.
D. At midnight on 25 May.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章明确提到 'Hours later, they reached the top of the towering mountain at 4:20 on 25 May.'

readingQuestions.stem: Which of the following statements is TRUE according to the passage?
options: A. All four climbers reached the top of the mountain.
B. The Second Step was easy to climb.
C. Qu Yinhua was the first one to climb up the human ladder.
D. They had enough oxygen for everyone.
readingQuestions.answer: C. Qu Yinhua was the first one to climb up the human ladder.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章提到 'He lowered his body and let Qu Yinhua stand on his shoulders.' 且最后提到刘连满没有登顶，其他人无氧登顶。
